note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Maps.lean:226 — abbrev induce (s : Set V) (G : SimpleGraph V) : SimpleGraph s, docstring: 'the subgraph induced by the given vertex set, a wrapper around comap'.
verify_note: Confirmed at Maps.lean:226; docstring 'restrict a graph to the vertices in s, deleting all edges incident to vertices outside the set' matches Wikidata Q24743362 description verbatim ('graph formed from a subset of vertices and all edges connecting pairs of vertices in that subset').

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Schnirelmann.lean:55 — noncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] : ℝ, the standard |A ∩ [1,n]|/n limit-inf definition.
verify_note: Confirmed at Schnirelmann.lean:55; def is literally the infimum of |A∩(0,n]|/n over n>0, matching Q2482753's 'measure how dense a sequence of numbers is' definition exactly.

note: Mathlib/RingTheory/Radical/Basic.lean:148 def radical (a : M) : M := ∏ primes dividing a (to first power); Mathlib/RingTheory/Radical/NatInt.lean specializes/computes it for ℕ and ℤ (radical_eq_prod_primeFactors) matching the Wikipedia notion exactly.
verify_note: Confirmed at Radical/Basic.lean:148 (namespace UniqueFactorizationMonoid); no separate Nat.radical/Int.radical def exists — NatInt.lean only adds computability lemmas about this same general radical specialized to M=ℤ/ℕ, so this general decl is the actual formal home; matches Q2498974's 'product of the prime factors of a given integer'.

note: Mathlib/Order/Defs/Unbundled.lean:324 — structure UpperSet (α) [LE α] where carrier : Set α; upper' : IsUpperSet carrier. Bundled type of upper sets, matches the order-theory concept.
verify_note: Confirmed at Defs/Unbundled.lean:324; extend_docs gives 'The type of upper sets of an order', and IsUpperSet is literally 'any element greater than one of its members is also a member', matching Q2528262's 'includes all successors of its elements' exactly.
